violations.category: Equipment, Food Contact Surfaces, and Utensils Clean
violations.observation: Observed quaternary ammonium compound solution utilized to sanitize food contact surfaces failing to meet parameters to adequately achieve sanitization. Location: Three-compartment sink. Concentration: 100 ppm. Temperature: 74 Observed utensil food-contact surface with the buildup of soil residues. Utensil Type: Prep table, soda dispenser holder, interior ice machine. Location: Front serving area. Condition of the Location: substantial soil and sticky buildup. ice machine with a brown like soil residue on the interior ceiling of the ice machine.

Observed wiping cloths improperly air drying in food service establishment. Location: Food contact surfaces. Manner: Wiping cloths stored on food contact surfaces prep tables and not stored in sanitizer buckets.
Observed Prep sink Hot and Cold faucet is not maintained in good repair or is not repaired by law. Observed Hot and cold valve front prep sink leaking.

Observed nonfood contact surface that was non corrosion resistant. The surface was located Backroom area . The surface was made out of a corrosion metal The condition of the surface was ice making units exterior with substantial rust buildup.
Observed the waste storage area to be unclean. Location: outside receptacle of the waste storage area Condition of the storage area: Substantial debris and soil buildup.
Observed that food employees were not in possession of a food handler card or had been trained in another approved food safety course.
Observed no testing kit or other device to measure the concentration of chemical sanitizing solution.
Observed that the facility was operating with an expired permit.

Observed wall behind door in food preparation area is not easily cleanable. Observed a large hole in the wall and a section of the wall cut out and removed exposing the interior area of wall and piping underneath the three-compartment sink at the time of inspection. HOMEWOOD SUITES ALBUQUERQUE UPTOWN - 7101 ARVADA AVE NE – Conditional Approved HOMEWOOD SUITES ALBUQUERQUE UPTOWN - 7101 ARVADA AVE NE Food Establishment Class C Date: 07/02/2026 - #: CAC-Food-062758-2026 - Inspection Result: Conditional Approved Facility was not Closed due to PIC correcting one Priority violation and three Priority Foundation on site bringing score back up to a yellow sticker Conditional Approved status. CHPD inspector instructed PIC to send photo documentation of corrections and repairs to contact information provided on business card. CHPD inspector advised PIC to obtain one Food Protection Manager Certification for the facility and all food employees must obtain food handlers cards within thirty days. Facility will remain under and yellow sticker Conditional Approved status until all violations have been corrected and repaired and photo documentation has provided and reviewed. PIC was issued a Corrective Action Compliance Report on site. CHPD inspector provided PIC with temp logs, calibration logs, educational flyers for food safety and employee health. Violations corrected: 1. Food Contact Surfaces Cleaned & Sanitized: Equipment, Food-Contact Surfaces and Utensils - Clean to Sight and Touch 2. Food Identification and Honestly Presented: Package Integrity 3. Handwashing Facilities: Using a Handwashing Sink-Operation and Maintenance 4. Handwashing Facilities: Hand Drying Provision HOMEWOOD SUITES ALBUQUERQUE UPTOWN - 7101 ARVADA AVE NE Food Establishment Class C Date: 07/02/2026 - #: RT-FOODEST-055442-2025 - Inspection Result: Closure Re-Inspection Required

Your Move LLC DBA Empire Board Game Library - 3503 CENTRAL AVE NE Food Establishment Class C Date: 06/30/2026 - #: CAC-Food-062153-2026 - Inspection Result: Unsatisfactory Re-Inspection required Facility will remain closed while PIC works on correcting and repairing observed violations. PIC must pay overdue invoices and or set up a payment plan with the consumer health division before facility is re-inspected. CHPD inspector instructed PIC to send photo documentation of corrections and repairs to contact information provided on business card. PIC must call for re-inspection. Facility will be reopened under an Unsatisfactory status as per city ordinance. Facility was not closed at time of inspection due to PIC correcting two Priority and two Priority Foundation violations on site bringing score back into an Unsatisfactory status. PIC was issued a Corrective Action Compliance Report allowing facility to remain open for operation. Violations corrected: 1. Cold Holding: Time/Temperature Control for Safety Food, Cold Holding 2.
